comment_text.sn3.16:1.5: He had hoped for a son. The girl was probably princess Vajirī, for whom the king expresses his love at MN 87:24.1. This event must have been several years before the war between Kosala and Magadha, for the commentaries say she was married to Ajātasattu upon cessation of hostilities.
comment_text.sn3.16:3.2: Read posā, which the commentary glosses posehi, i.e. ablative plural in comparative sense, “than men”.
comment_text.sn3.16:3.3: Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 6.4.17 gives a rite for those who wish to have a daughter who lives a full life and is an astute scholar (paṇḍitā).
comment_text.sn3.16:3.4: The compound sassudeva is attributive, “(she who has) mother-in-law as queen”. There is a subtle intertextual tragedy here, for her future mother-in-law was her aunt, who died because of the actions of her future husband Ajātasattu.
comment_text.sn3.16:4.4: According to tradition this did turn out to be the case, as her son with Ajātasattu was Udāyibhadda, fondly recalled by the king at DN 2:12.3. But like his father, he ascended the throne by regicide (Mahāvaṁsa 4.1).

root_text.sn3.16:1.2: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn3.16:1.3: Atha kho aññataro puriso yena rājā pasenadi kosalo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā rañño pasenadissa kosalassa upakaṇṇake ārocesi:
root_text.sn3.16:1.5: Evaṁ vutte, rājā pasenadi kosalo anattamano ahosi.
root_text.sn3.16:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ anattamanataṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn3.16:1.2: Then King Pasenadi of Kosala went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side.
translation_text.sn3.16:1.3: Then a man went up to the king and whispered in his ear,
translation_text.sn3.16:1.4: “Your Majesty, Queen Mallikā has given birth to a daughter.”
translation_text.sn3.16:1.5: When this was said, King Pasenadi was disappointed.
translation_text.sn3.16:2.1: Then, knowing that King Pasenadi was disappointed, on that occasion the Buddha recited these verses:
translation_text.sn3.16:3.1: “Well, some women are better than men,
translation_text.sn3.16:3.3: Wise and virtuous, a devoted wife
translation_text.sn3.16:4.1: And when she has a son,
translation_text.sn3.16:4.2: he becomes a hero, O lord of the land.
translation_text.sn3.16:4.3: The son of such a blessed lady

reference_text.sn3.16:1.1: cck15.118, ms12S1_672, msdiv127, ndp12.85, pts-vp-pli1ed1.86, pts-vp-pli2ed1.194, sya15.125
